title: worker: async/Promise-returning port silently mis-graded as DataCloneError
description: worker.ts:146 calls 'const value = fn(...inputArgs)' synchronously. If the port is async or returns a Promise, structured-clone of the Promise throws DataCloneError on postMessage. The grader sees an unhelpful 'could not be cloned' error instead of the real 'port violates sync contract' diagnosis. Docstring L13-14 asserts sync contract but does not enforce it. Major because sonnet L3 frequently emits async ports — the failure mode disguises itself as a serialization bug, masking the real defect. Fix: detect thenable post-call (typeof value.then === 'function') and throw 'port returned a Promise; sync contract violated' inside the try block.
close_reason: Fixed in Step 4 triage: 3-file edit pass, tsc clean. See Step 5 followup mpfr-ts-637.

title: Step 5 followup: verify partial harness files + complete runner.ts
description: Step 5 (mpfr-ts-odi.5) was stopped mid-stream due to session-end. eval/harness/value_codec.ts, ast_check.ts, worker.ts exist; runner.ts does not. Next agent: (1) Read all three for senior-TS quality issues; (2) verify they align with the Step 5 brief (esp. value_codec ExpectedOutput shape, worker keep-alive pattern, ast_check Cyrillic detection); (3) write runner.ts per spec; (4) execute the full acceptance suite (stub correct port pass, broken port fail, infloop terminates fast, schema violation rejected, NaN equality OK). Only close mpfr-ts-odi.5 after acceptance is GREEN.
close_reason: Step 5 complete: harness skeleton (value_codec, ast_check, worker, runner) + RED→GREEN acceptance suite, 5/5 scenarios passing, tsc clean. 9 audit bugs fixed pre-acceptance.

title: Step 10: Live sonnet L3 attempt + finalize
description: Render the mpn_add_n prompt via python3 eval/driver/ralph.py --function mpn_add_n. Save to /tmp/eval_pilot_mpn_add_n/prompt.txt. Orchestrator (parent Claude session) spawns ONE Agent(subagent_type=general-purpose, model=sonnet) with the prompt as task; agent writes /tmp/eval_pilot_mpn_add_n/port.ts. After agent returns, run bun eval/harness/runner.ts to grade; ast_check the port for core.ts import compliance; INSERT into runs table; UPDATE functions row. Accept: composite_correctness ≥ 0.95 AND ast_check passes AND state.db row reflects the run. If composite < 0.95 → file bd bug describing failure mode (Cyrillic? schema-violation? wrong carry? infinite loop?), HALT pilot per PIL.1. If composite ≥ 0.95 → write docs/worklog/001-pilot-mpn-add-n.md retrospective. PIL.5 is met only when ALL 10 pilot fns reach this state — this is just the first.
close_reason: Pilot Steps 6-10 complete for mpn_add_n. golden 152 cases, ref ports verified (correct=1.0 / broken=0.395 / gap=0.605), prompts.py + ralph.py written, sonnet L3 attempt scored 1.0/152. state.db updated. See docs/worklog/002-pilot-mpn-add-n.md. Remaining 9 pilot functions to be scaled out next.

title: Step 9: Prompt template + dry-run driver
description: Create eval/driver/prompts.py: build_prompt(run_info) emits the L3 sonnet prompt — embeds src/core.ts verbatim (Law 4 enforcement), embeds the C source from mpfr/src/ (or GMP equivalent for mpn fns), embeds spec.json contents, embeds the iteration loop instructions (write port to <path>, run bun eval/harness/runner.ts --function X --port <path> --golden <path> --output <path>, read grade.json, iterate up to 6 times, stop when composite ≥ 0.95). Create eval/driver/ralph.py with --dry-run flag that prints the rendered prompt without spawning an Agent, --function flag to pick which function. Accept: python3 eval/driver/ralph.py --function mpn_add_n --dry-run prints a complete prompt containing the words 'MPFR' (from core.ts), 'mpn_add_n' (from spec), the C signature, and the iteration loop instructions; prompt size between 5KB and 50KB.
close_reason: Pilot Steps 6-10 complete for mpn_add_n. golden 152 cases, ref ports verified (correct=1.0 / broken=0.395 / gap=0.605), prompts.py + ralph.py written, sonnet L3 attempt scored 1.0/152. state.db updated. See docs/worklog/002-pilot-mpn-add-n.md. Remaining 9 pilot functions to be scaled out next.

title: Step 8: Mutation-prove the golden
description: Run bun eval/harness/runner.ts twice: once against correct/mpn_add_n.ts, once against broken/mpn_add_n.ts. Record grade.json from both. Accept: correct composite_correctness ≥ 0.99 (ideally 1.0); broken composite_correctness ≤ 0.5; gap ≥ 0.49. If correct < 0.99 → the golden is wrong (or the reference port is wrong) — debug before continuing. If broken > 0.5 → the golden is insufficient (not catching the seeded bug) — add adversarial cases until it does. This is THE harness-soundness gate of the pilot.
close_reason: Pilot Steps 6-10 complete for mpn_add_n. golden 152 cases, ref ports verified (correct=1.0 / broken=0.395 / gap=0.605), prompts.py + ralph.py written, sonnet L3 attempt scored 1.0/152. state.db updated. See docs/worklog/002-pilot-mpn-add-n.md. Remaining 9 pilot functions to be scaled out next.

title: Step 7: Reference ports (correct + broken)
description: Create eval/reference_ports/correct/mpn_add_n.ts (hand-written, faithful, well-cited per Law 1) and eval/reference_ports/broken/mpn_add_n.ts (deliberately wrong: skip the carry propagation past index 0, or return carry as always 0n). NOTE: mpn_add_n is a substrate fn — it does NOT use the MPFR value type, so the core.ts import requirement doesn't apply. The spec is bigint[] in, {sum:bigint[], carry:bigint} out. Cite GMP §8.3 for limb endianness. Both files should be runnable via: bun eval/reference_ports/correct/mpn_add_n.ts (with a 3-line smoke import at the bottom guarded by import.meta.main). Accept: both files type-check, both export mpn_add_n with the spec signature, correct version handles single-limb and multi-limb correctly when tested by hand at REPL.
close_reason: Pilot Steps 6-10 complete for mpn_add_n. golden 152 cases, ref ports verified (correct=1.0 / broken=0.395 / gap=0.605), prompts.py + ralph.py written, sonnet L3 attempt scored 1.0/152. state.db updated. See docs/worklog/002-pilot-mpn-add-n.md. Remaining 9 pilot functions to be scaled out next.

title: Step 6: mpn_add_n spec + golden_driver + golden.jsonl
description: Create eval/functions/mpn_add_n/{spec.json, golden_driver.c}. spec.json: name=mpn_add_n, class=substrate, c_signature='mp_limb_t mpn_add_n(mp_limb_t *rp, const mp_limb_t *s1p, const mp_limb_t *s2p, mp_size_t n)', ts_signature='export function mpn_add_n(a: bigint[], b: bigint[], n: number): {sum: bigint[], carry: bigint}', deps=[], notes (limb endianness, carry semantics, n>=1 invariant). golden_driver.c: includes common.h, generates ~180 cases — happy (n=1..5, random limbs, 30 cases), edge (n=1 zero, all-ones max carry chain, single high bit, MSB of each limb, n=20 cascading carry, 40 cases), adversarial (n=50 all-0xff to force max carry, n=10 with carry-out at boundary, 20 cases), fuzz (deterministic xorshift, n in {1,2,3,5,10,20,50}, 90 cases). Each case emits {tag, inputs:{a:["123",...], b:[...], n:5}, output:{sum:[...], carry:"1"}, time_ns}. Accept: bash eval/golden_master/build.sh succeeds; bash run produces golden.jsonl with wc -l ≥ 150; all 4 tags present (grep -c '"tag":"happy"' ≥ 20, edge ≥ 30, adversarial ≥ 10, fuzz ≥ 50).
close_reason: Pilot Steps 6-10 complete for mpn_add_n. golden 152 cases, ref ports verified (correct=1.0 / broken=0.395 / gap=0.605), prompts.py + ralph.py written, sonnet L3 attempt scored 1.0/152. state.db updated. See docs/worklog/002-pilot-mpn-add-n.md. Remaining 9 pilot functions to be scaled out next.

title: Step 5: Harness skeleton (Bun worker pool)
description: Create eval/harness/{runner.ts, worker.ts, value_codec.ts, ast_check.ts} using Bun Worker API. runner.ts: parse args (--function, --port, --golden, --output), spawn worker pool of N=4, dispatch one case per message, class-tier hard wall (substrate=50ms, arithmetic=50ms, transcendental=200ms, misc=1s), worker.terminate() on timeout; aggregate composite per CLAUDE.md formula (0.6 corr + 0.2 edge + 0.2 mined; perf grade SEPARATE; composite=0 if compiled=false). worker.ts: imports port via dynamic import, calls fn with parsed args, returns {result, ms}. value_codec.ts: decodeMpfrValue(obj)->MPFR per src/core.ts; encodeForCompare(MPFR)->canonical JSON. ast_check.ts: parse port.ts string; verify 'import {...MPFR...} from "...core.ts"' present; verify no 'interface MPFR' or 'type RoundingMode' declarations; return {ok, errors}. Accept: smoke test against a stub port + stub golden (10 cases, all should pass) produces grade.json with composite=1.0; against a stub port with one wrong return produces composite<1.
notes: PARTIAL — agent stopped mid-step. Files written: eval/harness/{ast_check.ts (9KB), value_codec.ts (19KB), worker.ts (6KB)}. Files NOT written: eval/harness/runner.ts. Acceptance tests NOT run. Next session must (a) inspect the three written files for senior-TS quality, (b) write runner.ts per Step 5 brief, (c) run the FULL acceptance suite from the brief — especially the infinite-loop test. The brief is preserved as bd issue mpfr-ts-odi.5 description; re-read in full before continuing.
close_reason: Step 5 complete: harness skeleton (value_codec, ast_check, worker, runner) + RED→GREEN acceptance suite, 5/5 scenarios passing, tsc clean. 9 audit bugs fixed pre-acceptance.

title: Step 4: Golden-driver common.h
description: Create eval/golden_master/common.h adapted from ../auto-port-eval/eval/golden_master/common.h: keep xs64_seed/xs64_next/xs64_below PRNG, now_ns, jl_begin/jl_end_inputs/jl_finish JSONL helpers. ADD: jl_limb_array(f, key, limbs, n) — emits '"key":["123","456",...]' for mpn arrays (decimal strings to keep BigInt parse trivial). ADD: jl_mpfr_value(f, key, x) — emits MPFR value as JSON object (kind, sign, prec, exp_str, mant_hex via mpfr_get_str base 16). Include gmp.h and mpfr.h. Accept: 10-line test driver compiles + emits one valid JSON line that round-trips through Bun's JSON.parse.

title: Step 3: State DB schema + init
description: Create eval/driver/schema.sql with the three tables from CLAUDE.md §'State DB schema': functions, runs, cases (verbatim). Apply via sqlite3 eval/state.db < eval/driver/schema.sql. Insert one row: functions.name='mpn_add_n', class='substrate', signature='(a: bigint[], b: bigint[], n: number) => {sum: bigint[], carry: bigint}', deps='[]', status='pending', topo_rank=0. Accept: sqlite3 eval/state.db 'SELECT name, class, status FROM functions' shows mpn_add_n|substrate|pending.

title: Step 2: Locked schema src/core.ts
description: Implement src/core.ts EXACTLY as specified in CLAUDE.md §'Library coherence': MPFR interface (kind, sign, prec, exp, mant), RoundingMode string union ('RNDN'|'RNDZ'|'RNDU'|'RNDD'|'RNDA'), Ternary type (-1|0|1), Result interface ({value, ternary}), MPFRError class with code 'EPREC'|'EROUND'|'EDOMAIN'. No Bun.* or node:* imports. Accept: bun -e "import('./src/core.ts').then(m=>console.log(Object.keys(m)))" lists MPFRError; tsc --noEmit src/core.ts passes.

title: Step 1: Repo skeleton + system deps
description: Create PHASE.md, package.json (engines: bun>=1.3, node>=22), bunfig.toml, tsconfig.json, .gitignore, dir tree (src/, src/internal/{mpn,mpfr}, eval/{functions,harness,golden_master,driver,reference_ports/{correct,broken},integration,reports}, docs/{worklog,adr}). Verify libmpfr-dev, libgmp-dev, gcc, sqlite3, python3+venv, bun>=1.3, node>=22. Clone https://gitlab.inria.fr/mpfr/mpfr.git depth=1 to ./mpfr/. Accept: bun --version, gcc --version, pkg-config --cflags mpfr, ls mpfr/src/add.c all succeed.

title: Pilot: vertical slice for mpn_add_n
description: Epic tracking the 10 steps of the Pilot phase. Deliverable: harness proven sound by grading mpn_add_n end-to-end. See docs/PILOT_PLAN.md.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: add git pre-commit hook to auto-run 'bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl'
description: Manual git commits (not via ralph.py --commit-batch / --ship) skip bd export, leaving local Dolt updates invisible to anyone but the local checkout. The 50→85 session lost visibility of 50 issues + 10 memories until the next agent flagged it. A simple pre-commit hook that runs 'bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl' and stages the result would prevent recurrence. Cost: ~10 min. Install path: .git/hooks/pre-commit (chmod +x). Document the install in CLAUDE.md §Beads Issue Tracker.

title: cleanup: rewrite absolute-path imports in promoted ports to relative paths
description: Sonnet writes ports to /tmp/eval_<fn>/port.ts using absolute paths like '/home/tobias/Projects/mpfr-ts/src/core.ts' because /tmp/core.ts doesn't exist. The orchestrator's promote step (--ship's _promote_port helper, or manual cp) doesn't rewrite these to relative paths. Affected files include at least src/ops/add1sp1.ts, add1sp1n.ts, add1sp2.ts, add1sp2n.ts, add1sp3.ts, sub1sp1.ts, sub1sp1n.ts, sub1sp2.ts, sub1sp2n.ts, sub1sp3.ts, setmin.ts, src/internal/mpfr/powerof2_raw.ts. Fix: post-promote script that rewrites absolute paths to project-relative ones, OR build the rewrite into --ship's _promote_port. Breaks the 'pure ESM portable to any consumer' contract of the library.

title: ralph.py --next: pending-deps policy (callgraph reveals transitive deps not yet ported)
description: callgraph.py shows e.g. mpfr_set deps=[mpfr_set4] and mpfr_set_prec deps=[mpfr_reallocate_func] — both deps are still pending. The existing 50 ports were prepped with deps inlined/stubbed by the prep agent. --next must decide: (a) skip functions with any pending dep (strictest; smaller pickable set), (b) allow if deps are 'in_flight' OR 'done', (c) allow if deps appear in callgraph at all (relies on prep to handle stubs). Recommend (a) for first cut, with --include-pending-deps escape hatch. Flagged during Phase A2 (mpfr-ts-e24).

title: Phase D: worklog 006 + scaling-to-75 memory + session close
description: Per CLAUDE.md §Session close: update state.db, write docs/worklog/006-scale-out-engine.md with throughput data and new bd issues, refresh memory snapshot (especially scaling-to-75 lessons), bd export, git push.
close_reason: Worklog 006 + HANDOFF + memory snapshot landed. 9 scaling learnings recorded. Final state pushed to remote.

title: Phase C: scale-out 50 → 75 functions via the engine
description: 5 batches of ~5 functions each using the scale-out engine, halt-on-failure. Targets: 15 easy accessors/set-get variants, then medium primitive arithmetic. Record per-batch throughput (orchestrator actions, wall time, sonnet iter histogram, token cost) for worklog 006.
close_reason: Phase C exceeded target: 70 → 85 done (10 above 75 goal). Four batches: 5+5+7+15 ports, all sonnet L3 one-shot composite=1.0. Engine throughput went from 1.2 actions/fn (early batches) to 0.33 actions/fn (mega-batch). --ship mode landed mid-phase. Eight scale-out learnings recorded.

title: Phase B: pilot scale-out engine on 4 easy functions
description: Smoke-test the scale-out engine end-to-end on mpfr_get_prec, mpfr_set_prec, mpfr_set, mpfr_swap. Measure orchestrator actions/batch (target ≤3). If hit, proceed to scale 50→75; if not, file diagnostic bds and tune. Halt-on-failure policy applies.
close_reason: 3/4 ported successfully (mpfr_get_prec, mpfr_setmax, mpfr_setmin all composite=1.0); 1/4 cleanly deferred (mpfr_abort_prec_max blocked on mpfr-ts-79u expected_throw harness support). state.db now shows 53 done, 1 blocked. Engine end-to-end works. Action count: 6 per 3-fn batch (~1 per fn); --ship mode (mpfr-ts-NEW2) would cut to 4.

title: Phase A3: extend ralph.py with --next / --grade / --commit-batch
description: Per worklog 005 §'Suggested ralph.py CLI surface': add three modes. (a) --next [--batch-size N] [--filter class=X] picks next N pending functions by topo_rank, seeds state.db rows, renders prompts to /tmp/eval_<fn>/prompt.txt. (b) --grade <fn1> <fn2> ... spot-grades each /tmp/eval_<fn>/port.ts, INSERTs runs row, UPDATEs functions row, exits 0 if all >= 0.95 or 1 with diagnostic on any fail. (c) --commit-batch <msg>: bd export + git add + commit + push. RED-GREEN TDD with unittest. Keep existing --dry-run and --list-pending working.
close_reason: ralph.py extended with --next/--grade/--commit-batch. 20 new pytest cases + 2 regression cases all GREEN (34/34 in driver/tests/). Smoke against production state.db seeded 4 pending rows correctly; prep skeleton has all required workflow + mutation-prove invariants + hard constraints. Production state.db preserved (50 done, 4 pending added).

title: Phase A2: build eval/driver/callgraph.py
description: Per worklog 005 step 4: walk mpfr/src/*.c, regex-extract mpfr_* and mpn_* callsites, build caller→callees map, topo-sort, emit eval/driver/callgraph.json. Output schema: {function: {deps: [...], class: 'arithmetic|conversion|transcendental|misc|substrate', topo_rank: int}}. RED-GREEN TDD: write Python unit tests against a small fixture C file with known calls; assert deps + topo order correct. Heuristic regex is acceptable (clang AST is overkill for Pilot). Functions not yet in state.db are candidates for the next batch.
close_reason: callgraph.py + tests + smoke run all GREEN. 525 functions extracted in 0.67s; cycles handled; class detection works. callgraph.json written. Three follow-up bds filed for --next consumption policy.

title: Phase A1: fix ast_check false positive for 'import { type X }'
description: Per worklog 005 step 3: strip import statements before applying REDECL_PATTERNS in eval/harness/ast_check.ts so the mixed type-import syntax stops triggering MPFR-redeclaration. RED-GREEN TDD: add failing acceptance case to eval/acceptance/step5/ports/ (or unit test) showing 'import { type MPFR, RoundingMode, Result } from "../core.ts"' is currently flagged; then fix; then confirm. Re-run 5/5 acceptance and re-grade a known-good port (e.g. src/ops/mpfr_init2.ts) to confirm no regression. Resolves mpfr-ts-wli.
close_reason: ast_check.ts now strips import statements before applying REDECL_PATTERNS. 18 unit tests pass; 5/5 acceptance still green; mpfr_init2 still composite=1.0.

title: --next: public-API vs internal-helper priority not encoded; engine picks internals first
description: callgraph.json puts internal helpers (mpfr_abort_prec_max, mpfr_setmax, mpfr_setmin, sub1sp variants, powerof2_raw) at lowest topo_rank because they have no mpfr_* deps. But the existing 50-port pattern is to inline internals into the public API call (mpfr_add inlines add1/add1sp/sub1/sub1sp logic). So engine-natural ordering and project-natural ordering diverge. Decision needed: either (a) add a --public-only or --skip-internal flag to ralph.py --next, or (b) maintain a public-API allowlist file (eval/driver/public_api.txt) and filter against it, or (c) accept the engine's order and port internals as src/internal/mpfr/. Recommend (b) — a hand-curated allowlist gives the orchestrator control without needing to bake heuristics. Flagged during Phase A3 smoke test; Phase B will produce empirical evidence.

title: prompt: public-surface ports need import-path guidance for /tmp/ grading
description: When an agent writes a port to /tmp/eval_<fn>/port.ts, the relative import 'import { X } from "../core.ts"' resolves to /tmp/core.ts (non-existent). Sonnet's mpfr_init2 attempt failed iter 1 with this exact error, recovered iter 2 by using absolute path '/home/tobias/Projects/mpfr-ts/src/core.ts'. The prompt template (eval/driver/prompts.py) should: (a) instruct the agent to use the absolute path for /tmp/ ports, and (b) note that the orchestrator will rewrite to relative when promoting to canonical (src/ops/...). Burns one iteration unnecessarily on every public port until fixed. P2 — affects every non-substrate port from here on.
close_reason: Fixed in prompts.py: public-port instructions now include absolute-path import guidance. Verified: rendered prompt for mpfr_init2 shows the two-statement import pattern with absolute path.

title: ast_check: 'import { type MPFR }' falsely flagged as MPFR redeclaration
description: The REDECL_PATTERNS regex (?:^|[^.\w])(?:interface|type)\s+MPFR\b matches inside 'import { type MPFR } from "../core.ts"' — '{' is non-word char before 'type', and ' MPFR ' satisfies '\s+MPFR\b' (word boundary before '}'). False positive: import type {...} syntax is one of two ways to import a type-only symbol; flagging it would force every public port to use 'import type { MPFR } from ...' separately (which is what mpfr_init2's prep worked around). Fix: strip import statements before applying REDECL_PATTERNS (mirror the comment/string strip we did for ANY_PATTERNS, issue mpfr-ts-4hp). Test: 'import { type MPFR } from "../core.ts"' should NOT be flagged; 'interface MPFR {}' or 'type MPFR = number' should still be flagged.
close_reason: ast_check.ts now strips import statements before applying REDECL_PATTERNS. 18 unit tests pass; 5/5 acceptance still green; mpfr_init2 still composite=1.0.

title: runner.ts: relative --port / --golden paths resolve against eval/harness/, not cwd
description:
When a user invokes `bun eval/harness/runner.ts --port src/internal/mpn/add_n.ts ...` from the repo root, the worker fails to import the port because `pathToUrl` resolves relative paths against `import.meta.url` (which points at `eval/harness/runner.ts`) instead of against the cwd.

Reproduce (from repo root):
  bun eval/harness/runner.ts \
    --function mpn_add_n \
    --port src/internal/mpn/add_n.ts \
    --golden eval/functions/mpn_add_n/golden.jsonl \
    --output /tmp/g.json --class substrate \
    --spec eval/functions/mpn_add_n/spec.json

Observed: grade.json first_error reads:
  'port init exceeded 2000ms... Cannot find module
   /home/tobias/Projects/mpfr-ts/eval/harness/src/internal/mpn/add_n.ts'

i.e. the runner glued `src/internal/mpn/add_n.ts` onto `eval/harness/` rather than the cwd.

Workaround: pass absolute paths (works).

Root cause: `pathToUrl()` near runner.ts L988 builds `new URL(p, cwdUrl)` where `cwdUrl = new URL('./', import.meta.url)`. That is NOT the cwd — it's the runner's own directory. The fix is to resolve relative paths against `process.cwd()` (Bun exposes this).

Surfaced during Pilot Step 7 (mpn_add_n reference ports). Step 7 worked around it by passing absolute paths; the orchestrator + ralph loop need to either always pass absolute paths or this needs to be fixed in the runner. Fixing in the runner is the right answer — relative paths from cwd are the natural CLI ergonomics.

Acceptance: from repo root, the reproducer above produces composite=1.0 against src/internal/mpn/add_n.ts and the step5 acceptance still passes 5/5.
close_reason: Fixed: pathToUrl now resolves against process.cwd() not import.meta.url. Verified: bun runner.ts --port src/internal/mpn/add_n.ts (relative) gives composite=1.0, 152/152 PASS. Step 5 acceptance suite still GREEN.

title: ast_check: default-from-core import counted as valid coreImport
description: ast_check.ts:174-178 records 'default(name)' in coreImports for 'import Foo from "../core.ts"'. core.ts has no default export — the docstring at L82-84 acknowledges this is 'always a bug'. But because the token is pushed into coreImports, the requireCoreImport gate (L180) sees length>0 and passes. A port that imports nothing real from core slips the gate. Fix: either emit an explicit schema error for default-from-core, or do not count 'default(...)' toward satisfying requireCoreImport.
close_reason: Fixed in Step 4 triage: 3-file edit pass, tsc clean. See Step 5 followup mpfr-ts-637.

title: value_codec: malformed Result silently falls through to object decoding
description: In decodeExpectedOutput (value_codec.ts:261-275), the Result-shape guard requires looksLikeMpfrWire(value) AND isTernary(ternary). If both conditions FAIL — e.g. golden has {value: <wire>, ternary: 2} — the dispatch falls through to the generic 'object' branch, decoding it as a struct with arbitrarily-decoded value/ternary fields. The grader then mis-classifies the shape downstream rather than surfacing 'malformed golden'. Senior-TS posture is fail-loud at the trust boundary: if value is MpfrWire-shaped and a ternary field is present, require isTernary(ternary) and throw otherwise.
close_reason: Fixed in Step 4 triage: 3-file edit pass, tsc clean. See Step 5 followup mpfr-ts-637.

title: value_codec: BigInt(non-integer number) crashes comparator
description: value_codec.ts:387 and :468 use Number.isFinite to gate BigInt(actual) coercion, but isFinite admits non-integer floats. BigInt(3.14) throws RangeError, crashing the worker instead of producing a clean 'scalar mismatch'. A port that mistakenly returns a float where a u64 is expected surfaces as a worker error, not a graded mismatch — masking the real defect. Fix: gate on Number.isInteger and return a mismatch string for non-integer numbers.
close_reason: Fixed in Step 4 triage: 3-file edit pass, tsc clean. See Step 5 followup mpfr-ts-637.

title: ralph.py --grade: resolve_port_path fallback doesn't check src/internal/{mpfr,mpn}/ for substrate
description: --grade falls back from /tmp/eval_<fn>/port.ts to src/ops/<short>.ts but NOT src/internal/mpfr/<short>.ts or src/internal/mpn/<short>.ts. Substrate functions (class='substrate') fail with 'no port file' even when correctly promoted. Workaround: use --class substrate and manual runner invocation, OR re-run --grade with /tmp port present. Fix: extend resolve_port_path to read state.db's class column and choose the right canonical path. Surfaced Phase C Batch 3 (mpfr_powerof2_raw).

title: Phase C batch 2: mpfr_set4 + mpfr_sub1sp1n broken-port gaps in [0.45, 0.55] danger zone
description: Per HANDOFF lesson #20 (broken-port calibration is brittle near 0.5): mpfr_set4 broken=0.4847, mpfr_sub1sp1n broken=0.4455. Both pass the < 0.55 threshold but a future weaker perturbation could regress to 0.55+. Strengthen these (e.g. inflate adversarial cases targeting the broken branch) so the gap is clearly < 0.45. Accepting for now since correct=1.0 and broken < 0.5; revisit before Phase D. Other 3 sub1sp variants (sub1sp2/2n/3) have clean gaps 0.22-0.26.

title: harness: value_codec can't decode bare-string scalars (wrapped {name:str} workaround in print_rnd_mode)
description: value_codec.ts L274's decodeExpectedOutput accepts decimal-integer, NaN/Infinity sentinels, and IEEE-double-shape strings but throws on identifier-style strings. mpfr_print_rnd_mode (Phase C batch 1) wrapped its output as {name: string} to route comparison through the harness's 'object' branch. Cleaner: add a 'scalar-string' branch to decodeExpectedOutput so future ports like mpfr_get_str / mpfr_version can return strings directly. Surfaced Phase C batch 1.

title: grader: enforce CLAUDE.md Rule 7 tag minimums (currently documented, not enforced)
description: CLAUDE.md Rule 7 says: 'A golden with only happy cases is broken and blocks the function. The grader rejects such goldens with error: insufficient golden coverage.' But runner.ts doesn't actually check tag counts — Phase C batch 1 had mpfr_add1sp1 with edge=27 (below 30 minimum) and the grader still scored composite=1.0. Add an enforcement check at runner.ts load-time: parse the golden, count by tag, reject if any class is below the documented minimum (happy>=20, edge>=30, adversarial>=10, fuzz>=50, mined>=5). Surfaced Phase C batch 1.

title: state.db topo_rank vs callgraph topo_rank: stale, decide on source of truth
description: state.db's topo_rank was hand-assigned for the original 50 functions (0..49 contiguous). callgraph.json computes topo_rank against the full 525-function graph (0..~500). When --next seeds new functions, it should populate state.db.topo_rank from callgraph. The 50 existing rows' ranks are stale but harmless (the column is only used by --next, and --next only looks at status='pending'). Optionally backfill the 50 done rows from callgraph for consistency. Flagged during Phase A2.

title: callgraph: mpn_* substrate fns are defined in GMP, not in mpfr/src/
description: mpn_add_n, mpn_sub_n, mpn_cmp, mpn_lshift are in state.db (status=done, substrate) but absent from eval/driver/callgraph.json because their definitions live in libgmp upstream, not in mpfr/src/. ralph.py --next should treat mpn_* deps as 'external/satisfied' when the substrate port already exists under src/internal/mpn/. Production-phase: optionally point callgraph at a GMP source clone too. Flagged during Phase A2.

title: bunfig.toml: 'preload = []' breaks 'bun test' when invoked from eval/harness/
description: Bun 1.3.13 rejects 'preload = []' (empty array) at parse time with 'Expected preload to be an array' when the cwd is eval/harness/. Surprising because the value IS an array. Workaround: cd to a different directory and pass an absolute path to bun test. Fix: remove the empty preload key from bunfig.toml, or move to TOML 'null'/inline syntax. Surfaced during Phase A1 (mpfr-ts-cbd) ast_check test development. Low-priority for now (workaround works) but cheap to fix and could bite anyone running tests from sub-cwds.

title: engine: add ralph.py --ship <msg> that runs --grade --promote and --commit-batch atomically
description: Phase B end-to-end measured 6 orchestrator actions per 3-fn batch: --next, prep, sonnets, --grade, manual promote, --commit-batch. A '--ship <msg> <fn1> <fn2> ...' mode that (a) grades each /tmp port, (b) iff all pass, copies /tmp/eval_<fn>/port.ts to src/ops/<short>.ts, (c) runs --commit-batch — collapses 3 actions into 1. Failure semantics: if any grade <0.95, no promote, no commit, stderr lists failures. Target: 4 actions per batch (--next, prep, sonnets, --ship). bd: mpfr-ts-1jr Phase B learning #5.
close_reason: --ship mode landed. 52/52 ralph tests pass including 17 new --ship tests. Cuts orchestrator actions per batch from 6 to 4.

title: Phase B: blocked on grader-throws — abort_prec_max deferred until mpfr-ts-NEW1
description: mpfr_abort_prec_max deferred per Phase B prep decision (b). Blocked until expected_throw support lands. State.db row marked status='blocked'. Re-attempt after harness extension.

title: harness: add eval/golden_master/run_all.sh to materialise all golden.jsonl files
description: CLAUDE.md §Build references 'bash eval/golden_master/run_all.sh' but the file doesn't exist. Each prep run today loops manually: for fn in eval/functions/*/; do $fn/golden_driver > $fn/golden.jsonl; done. Add a clean wrapper that mirrors build.sh's iteration and emits progress per-function. Saves a per-batch script in every prep prompt. Surfaced during Phase B prep (mpfr-ts-1jr).

title: harness: add expected_throw wire-codec support for cases that must raise
description: Required by mpfr_abort_prec_max (which calls abort()) and any future port whose contract is to raise (e.g. domain errors). Add an 'expected_throw: {code: string, message?: string}' wire tag in the golden JSONL. The grader should classify a thrown MPFRError matching the code as n_pass; an unthrown or mismatched-code case as n_throw. Touches: eval/harness/runner.ts, eval/harness/value_codec.ts, eval/harness/worker.ts, eval/golden_master/common.h (new jl_kv_expected_throw helper). ~150-200 LOC. Unblocks mpfr_abort_prec_max and grader-throws-for-domain-errors variants of asin/acos/log/sqrt-negative.

title: runner: n_throw counts output mismatches, not just port exceptions
description: runner.ts (around L960 per mpn_lshift prep subagent's reading) classifies output-comparison failures as 'threw: true', conflating real JS exceptions with wrong-but-clean returns. The composite formula is correct (still counts as not-pass), but diagnostics confuse readers: 'n_throw=150' suggests 150 exceptions when the port may not have raised once. Fix: split into n_throw (real exception) and n_mismatch (wrong-shape or wrong-value return). Update grade.json schema if needed (additive, won't break consumers). P3 — diagnostic-quality, not correctness.

title: state.db runs schema requires perf_grade NOT NULL but we don't measure perf yet
description: Inserting a run row requires perf_grade (REAL NOT NULL) and usd_est (REAL NOT NULL). Today we don't measure either. Setting both to 0.0 inserts cleanly but pollutes future perf-grade queries (e.g. 'slow-but-correct candidates' returns every function we've ever graded). Two options: (a) relax the NOT NULL on perf_grade/usd_est to allow NULL='not measured'; (b) wire a perf measurement path before more runs accumulate. (a) is the simpler fix; consider as part of scale-out. ADR if the schema changes meaningfully.

title: Worked-example-eval-leak: prompt embeds the target function for function #1
description: Pilot Step 10's L3 attempt on mpn_add_n returned composite=1.0 on iteration 1, but the prompt template (eval/driver/prompts.py) embeds src/internal/mpn/add_n.ts verbatim as the 'worked example'. For function #1, the worked example IS the target. The port is correct but the run does NOT test sonnet's algorithmic-porting capability — it tests transcription. Resolution path: for function #2+ the worked example continues to be mpn_add_n while the target differs; the leak is gone naturally. No code change needed today; flagging for the worklog and for the Pilot retrospective. Acceptance: file is the record; close once function #2 confirms 'real' porting.

title: worker: parent-side init-timeout requirement undocumented
description: A port with top-level await or top-level infinite work blocks 'await import(portUrl)' at worker.ts:107; worker never sends 'ready' or 'init_error'. Runner.ts must time-bound the init round-trip, but this contract is not stated in worker.ts. Docstring covers per-case timeout (L20-24) but not init. Fix: add one sentence to top-of-file docstring noting 'the parent must also time-bound the init → ready round-trip'. Pairs with runner.ts implementation requirement.
close_reason: Fixed in Step 4 triage: 3-file edit pass, tsc clean. See Step 5 followup mpfr-ts-637.

title: worker: unknown message type silently dropped, no exhaustiveness guard
description: worker.ts:103-163 has two if-branches and no else / never-assertion. A garbled/future message yields no reply; the parent's race timer eventually fires and the worker is killed without diagnostic. Senior-TS pattern: add 'const _exhaustive: never = msg' tail to lock exhaustiveness and post a diagnostic message when type doesn't match.
close_reason: Fixed in Step 4 triage: 3-file edit pass, tsc clean. See Step 5 followup mpfr-ts-637.

title: ast_check: any-pattern regexes scan comments and string literals
description: ANY_PATTERNS at ast_check.ts:113-118 match inside // comments, /** JSDoc */, and string literals. A port whose JSDoc mentions 'returns: any value' or contains a string 'prec: any bigint' would be falsely rejected as schema-violating. The Cyrillic scan over JSDoc is intentional (the predecessor scar was in a string literal), but the any-check is a different intent. Fix: strip line and block comments before applying ANY_PATTERNS, or document this gate as intentionally code+comment scoped.
close_reason: Fixed in Step 4 triage: 3-file edit pass, tsc clean. See Step 5 followup mpfr-ts-637.

title: ast_check: path glob accepts impostor *core.ts paths
description: Regexes at ast_check.ts:71, :77, :86 match [^'"]*core\.ts — accepts not_core.ts, evil-core.ts, fake-core.ts. Law 4 enforcement is bypassable by naming a file *core.ts. No such files exist today (latent gap). Fix: anchor with (?:^|/)core\.ts to require /core.ts or string-start core.ts.
close_reason: Fixed in Step 4 triage: 3-file edit pass, tsc clean. See Step 5 followup mpfr-ts-637.

title: value_codec: missing exhaustiveness check on ExpectedOutput switch
description: The switch on expected.kind at value_codec.ts:376 relies on each case returning to satisfy noImplicitReturns; it lacks the idiomatic 'default: const _exhaust: never = expected; throw ...' guard. If ExpectedOutput grows a 5th variant (e.g. when jl_output_scalar_str gets handled), the compiler will not warn at this site. Add the never-check.
close_reason: Fixed in Step 4 triage: 3-file edit pass, tsc clean. See Step 5 followup mpfr-ts-637.

title: Bug averted: bd-init .gitignore '*.db' glob would have excluded eval/state.db
description: During Step 1 (mpfr-ts-odi.1) the subagent noticed bd init's default .gitignore contained '*.db' which would have silently excluded eval/state.db — violating CLAUDE.md Rule 9 (state.db is the single source of truth and MUST be checked in). Fix: replaced glob with explicit ignores and a sentinel comment. Filing as closed for the historical record so future agents debugging 'why is state.db not in git' can find this.

title: Block all libc-alloc wrappers (allocate/free/reallocate) — immutable TS has no manual alloc
description: mpfr_allocate_func, mpfr_free_func, mpfr_reallocate_func wrap mp_get_memory_functions's malloc/free/realloc. Immutable TS API has no manual allocation — V8/Bun GC handles it. These functions have no semantic equivalent in the TS port. state.db rows status='blocked' so engine --next skips. Reattempt only if a future build mode introduces user-overridable allocators (very unlikely).

title: runner.ts: 1287 lines — consider golfing once we have port-throughput signal
description: runner.ts came in at 1287 lines (Step 6). Heavier than the 300-500 guidance. Currently clean (tsc passes, acceptance 5/5) but a senior TS engineer would likely get it to 800-1000 via better abstraction (e.g., extract worker-pool driver, extract composite aggregator, extract CLI arg parser). Defer until we have signal from real ports about which paths matter. Also includes 3 self-flagged concerns: (1) __malformed__ synthetic tag is filtered out of by_tag but counted in n_cases/n_throw; (2) substrate=arithmetic=50ms shared budget may need lifting if substrate routines bench slower; (3) inferParams() relies on JSON.parse preserving insertion order (true in V8/JSC/Bun, called out for the record).

value: Critical gotcha (2026-05-24): bd commands (create/close/remember) write to local Dolt only. .beads/issues.jsonl is the git-tracked source-of-truth but ONLY refreshes via 'bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl' which is only called automatically by ralph.py --commit-batch and --ship. Manual git commits skip the export, leaving 50+ bd entries invisible to teammates/next-agent. Fix: run 'bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl' before every git commit, or use --commit-batch/--ship which do it. Worth a git pre-commit hook.

Scale-out learning #10 (end-of-session user directive 2026-05-24): Next-session mega-batch target = 30 functions. Cost-discipline: ONE opus prep dispatch covering all 30, then sonnet dispatches in WAVES of 5-10 in parallel (NOT all 30 at once). Rationale: failed sonnets at full-parallel-dispatch burn ~50K tokens each; waves let each wave's signal inform the next, recovering cost when a prompt issue surfaces. Wave sequencing: dispatch → await → spot-check + --grade subset → next wave. Pick 30 from a coherent theme (suggested: conversion family) for maximum opus-prep amortization. After the final wave, single --ship --message handles the atomic commit+push.

Scale-out learning #2 (Phase A2): callgraph.py found 525 mpfr/mpn function defs in mpfr/src/, denser than the worklog's '~600' estimate. 8 cycles in MPFR upstream (mpfr_div, mpfr_pow_general/is_exact/pos_z/z, mpfr_digamma/reflection) — Tarjan-natural ordering survives them. Class distribution: misc=324, transcendental=121, arithmetic=38, conversion=34, substrate=3. The class heuristic is good enough for prompt-template selection but mpfr_sgn (predicate) shows up as 'arithmetic'; if we need a 'predicate' class later, that's a separate refinement.

Scale-out learning #3 (Phase A3): the engine's --next picker uses callgraph topo_rank, which puts INTERNAL helpers (mpfr_abort_prec_max, mpfr_setmin, mpfr_setmax, sub1sp variants) at rank 0-15 because they have no MPFR deps. The existing 50 ports inlined these internals into public-API ports rather than porting them separately. Strategic question for orchestrator: do we (a) port internals first and compose public API on them (callgraph-natural), or (b) maintain a public-surface allowlist and skip internals (project-policy-natural)? Phase B will validate which is the easier path. Worth a bd issue once empirical signal is in.

Scale-out learning #4 (Phase B prep): prep agent for a 4-function batch took ~199K tokens, ~17min wall, produced all deliverables for 3 portable functions + cleanly deferred 1 (abort_prec_max needs grader-throw-support). Mutation-prove gaps very clean (correct=1.0, broken=0.06-0.17). The prep agent's read-everything privileges + the worked-example corpus made this fast. Senior-eng takeaway: dispatching prep as ONE subagent for the batch (vs N subagents) gave the agent cross-function context (shared subtle bit: 'mpfr_setmax/setmin defined in mpfr-impl.h, not public mpfr.h').

Critical gotcha (2026-05-24): .gitignore 'mpfr/' without leading slash matches ANY mpfr/ dir, including src/internal/mpfr/. Always anchor patterns that collide with project dirs: '/mpfr/' not 'mpfr/'. Bit this project for 12+ hours, silently dropping 3 substrate files. Audit gitignore patterns when adding new dirs whose names collide with existing ignored patterns.

Scale-out learning #1 (Phase A1): senior-eng best practice for regex-based source-gate fixes is to preserve newlines when stripping, so downstream line-number-sensitive checks (e.g. the Cyrillic line reporter in ast_check.ts) stay aligned. The fix used .replace(/[^\n]/g, ' ') for the import strip; same approach should be applied any time we add new stripping passes. mpfr-ts-cbd resolved.

Scale-out learning #5 (Phase B end-to-end): Engine throughput with read-restricted sonnet — prep batched 4 funcs (~199K tokens, ~17min wall), 3 sonnets ran in parallel one-shot 1.0 (~45K tokens each, ~60s each), --grade independently verified 1.0 across all 3. Total orchestrator actions for the batch: 6 (--next, prep, parallel sonnets, --grade, promote, commit-batch). For 5-fn batches with parallel sonnets this scales to ~6 actions/batch, ~1.2/fn. The worklog ≤3 target needs collapsing --grade+promote+commit-batch (a '--ship' mode) and/or relaxing the prep-sonnet separation. Both improvements are bd candidates for Phase C.

Scale-out learning #9 (Phase C mega-batch result): 15 functions in one prep dispatch + 15 parallel sonnets + atomic --grade/promote/commit = 5 orchestrator actions for 15 ports (0.33/fn). All 15 sonnets one-shot 1.0. Worklog 005 cap was 8 parallel agents; 15 worked cleanly. Bigger batches dominate small ones on actions/fn; the bottleneck is no longer orchestrator action count but token cost and human review bandwidth. Recipe: hand-pick the batch (engine's strict callgraph picks internals first; user-facing functions are usually rank>=100), seed via INSERT, dispatch one opus prep, dispatch N parallel sonnets, --grade, promote with path rewrite, --commit-batch.

Scale-out learning #6 (user direction 2026-05-24): The bottleneck is orchestrator action count, not per-token cost. Opus is the right choice for prep when it produces best-quality output. Scale up by (a) bigger prep batches — push past 5-7 toward 20+ functions per prep dispatch; (b) parallel sonnet dispatches scoped only by max-parallel-agents limit; (c) collapse grade+promote+commit into --ship. Mantra: 'what would scale this up to bigger sets?'

Scale-out learning #7 (engine --ship): A single --ship --message MSG fn1 fn2 ... command does grade+promote+commit-batch atomically. Atomic semantics: any failure → no promote, no commit (but runs rows ARE inserted). Reduces orchestrator actions per N-fn batch from 6 to 4. With future SDK-direct sonnet dispatch this could go to 3.

Scale-out learning #8 (engine refinements during Phase C batch 3): (a) absolute-path imports from sonnet ports require post-promote rewrite or in-promote rewrite; current --ship doesn't do it (mpfr-ts-NEW). (b) --grade resolve_port_path doesn't fall back to src/internal/ for substrate functions (mpfr-ts-NEW). Both are small fixes; bundle into next engine pass. The ports themselves are correct — these are tooling gaps surfacing as batch size grows.
